atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.a:
| Reaction Step | Description |
|---|---|
| HF Exposure | HF reacts with AlCH₃* surface species to form AlF* surface species and CH₄ reaction product. HF also reacts with the underlying Al₂O₃ film to form an AlF₃ surface layer and H₂O as a reaction product. |
| TMA Exposure | TMA reacts with the AlF₃ surface layer on the Al₂O₃ substrate to form volatile AlF(CH₃)₂ reaction products. AlF₂(CH₃)* surface species may also be produced and removed by additional TMA exposure. |
atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.b:
| Reaction Step | Description |
|---|---|
| HF Exposure | HF reacts with AlCH₃* surface species to form AlF* surface species and CH₄ as a reaction product. HF also reacts with the underlying Al₂O₃ film to form an AlF₃ surface layer and H₂O as a reaction product. |
| TMA Exposure | TMA reacts with the AlF₃ surface layer on the Al₂O₃ substrate to form volatile AlF(CH₃)₂ reaction products. AlF₂(CH₃)* surface species may also be produced and removed by additional TMA exposure. |

atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.a:
| Step | Reaction |
|---|---|
| I | Adsorption (self-limiting) |
| II | Purge |
| III | Removal (self-limiting) |
| IV | Purge |
atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.b:
| Step | Reaction |
|---|---|
| I | Adsorption (self-limiting) |
| II | Purge |
| III | Adsorption (self-limiting) |
| IV | Purge |

atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.a:
| Step | Reaction |
|---|---|
| 1 | Fluorination: Al₂O₃ + HF → AlF₃ + H₂O |
| 2 | Ligand Exchange: AlF₃ + Al(CH₃)₃ → AlF(CH₃)₂ + Al(CH₃)₃ |
atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.b:
| Reaction Step | Reaction Description |
|---|---|
| 1 | HF reacts with Al₂O₃ surface to form AlF₃ and H₂O |
| 2 | TMA reacts with AlF₃ to form volatile AlF(CH₃)₂ and H₂O |
| 3 | AlF(CH₃)₂ desorbs from the surface |

atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.a:
| Reaction Step | Description |
|---|---|
| 1 | Oxidation of W thin film using O₃ to form WOₓ |
| 2 | Fluorination of WOₓ with HF to form volatile products |
| 3 | Desorption of volatile products, achieving net etching |
atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.b:
| Reaction Step | Description |
|---|---|
| 1 | Oxidation of TiN using O₃ to form TiO₂ |
| 2 | Fluorination of TiO₂ using HF to form volatile products |
atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.c:
| Reaction Step | Description |
|---|---|
| 1 | Oxidation of TiN using O₃ |
| 2 | Conversion of TiO₂ to B₂O₃ using BCl₃ |
| 3 | Fluorination of B₂O₃ by HF to form volatile products |

atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.a:
| Reaction Step | Description |
|---|---|
| I | Fluorination: HF reacts with Al₂O₃ surface to form AlF₃ |
| II | Purge: Inert gas removes excess HF and gaseous by-products (H₂O, CH₄, SnF(acac)) |
| III | Ligand-Exchange: TMA reacts with AlF₃ to form volatile AlF₃ and solid Al₂O₃ |
| IV | Purge: Inert gas removes excess TMA and gaseous by-products (CH₄, HF, AlF₃) |
atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.b:
| Reaction Step | Description |
|---|---|
| I | Fluorination: HF reacts with Al₂O₃ surface to form AlF₃ |
| II | Purge: Inert gas removes excess HF and gaseous by-products (H₂O, CH₄, SnF(acac)) |
| III | Ligand-Exchange: TMA reacts with AlF₃ to form volatile AlF₃·TMA complex |
| IV | Purge: Inert gas removes volatile by-products |

atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.a:
| Reaction Step | Chemical Equation |
|---|---|
| Conversion | 3SiO₂(s) + 4Al(CH₃)₃(g) → 2Al₂O₃(s) + 3Si(CH₃)₄(g) |
| Fluorination | Al₂O₃ + HF → AlF₃ + SiOₓFᵧ |
| Ligand Exchange | AlF₃ + SiOₓFᵧ + TMA → Al₂O₃ + volatile products |

| Reaction Step | Description |
|---|---|
| 1. Oxidation | TiN is oxidized to TiO₂ by O₃ |
| 2. Fluorination | TiO₂ is fluorinated to volatile TiF₄ by HF |
| 3. Removal | TiF₄ is removed from the surface |
atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.b:
| Reaction Step | Description |
|---|---|
| 1. Oxidation | TiN reacts with O₃ to form TiO₂ |
| 2. Fluorination | HF reacts with TiO₂ to form volatile TiF₄ and H₂O |
| 3. Removal | TiF₄ is removed from the surface |

atomic-layer-etching/experimental-usecas.a:
| Removal Energy (e.g., Ion Energy) | EPC (Å/cycle) |
|-----------------------------------|---------------|
| Regime I                          | Incomplete Removal |
| Regime II                         | ALE Window |
| Regime III                        | Sputtering |

atomic-layer-deposition/simulation-useca.c:
| Condition | Description |
|---|---|
| Pristine | Lines of Pt are visible, located on the grain boundaries of the graphene, confirming the enhanced reactivity of grain boundaries and defect sites towards MeCpPtMe3 adsorption. |
| 1 min O2 Plasma | Small Pt particles are visible all over the graphene surface. |
| 2 min O2 Plasma | More functional groups on the graphene surface and increases the coverage further after 500 Pt ALD cycles. |
| 3 min O2 Plasma | More functional groups on the graphene surface and increases the coverage further after 500 Pt ALD cycles. |
| 5 min O2 Plasma | A highly closed layer is obtained for the sample treated with 5 min O2 plasma, showing that it is indeed the creation of epoxide, hydroxide and carbonyl groups that enhances the nucleation. Only graphene wrinkles (folds in the graphene) are more difficult to cover with Pt (darker lines in the SEM images), and remain partially uncovered even after a 5 min O2 plasma treatment. |
atomic-layer-deposition/simulation-useca.d:
| Condition | Description |
|---|---|
| Pristine | Lines of Pt visible at grain boundaries |
| 1 min O₂ Plasma | Small Pt particles visible across surface |
| 2 min O₂ Plasma | Increased Pt coverage |
| 3 min O₂ Plasma | Further increased coverage |
| 5 min O₂ Plasma | Highly closed layer, wrinkles partially covered |
atomic-layer-deposition/simulation-useca.e:
| Condition | Description |
|---|---|
| Pristine | Lines of Pt visible at grain boundaries |
| 1 min O₂ Plasma | Small Pt particles visible across surface |
| 2 min O₂ Plasma | Increased Pt coverage |
| 3 min O₂ Plasma | Higher coverage observed |
| 5 min O₂ Plasma | Highly closed layer achieved |
atomic-layer-deposition/simulation-useca.f:
| Condition | Description |
|---|---|
| Pristine Graphene | Lines of Pt visible at grain boundaries |
| 1 min O₂ Plasma | Small Pt particles visible across surface |
| 2 min O₂ Plasma | Increased Pt coverage |
| 3 min O₂ Plasma | Higher Pt coverage |
| 5 min O₂ Plasma | Highly closed Pt layer, wrinkles partially uncovered |
